old_claim.note: Portolan Charts & Maritime Navigation provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency is retained between Charterparty Shipping Contracts and Portolan Charts & Maritime Navigation; A charterparty is a shipping contract and does not require portolan charts as a direct legal prerequisite.

source_shape.source_locator: Article defines a charter party as a contract by which a shipowner lets a ship or its cargo capacity to others; it does not require portolan charts.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: A charterparty is a shipping contract and does not require portolan charts as a direct legal prerequisite.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The source supports the scoped Charterparty Shipping Contracts claim or adjacent chronology without requiring Portolan Charts & Maritime Navigation as a prerequisite.

ontology_before: Charterparty Shipping Contracts treated Portolan Charts & Maritime Navigation as a direct prerequisite.
ontology_after: Charterparty Shipping Contracts no longer directly depends on Portolan Charts & Maritime Navigation; the dependency boundary is narrowed to operationally necessary predecessors.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Invariant changed: charterparty_shipping_contracts must not directly depend on portolan_charts_maritime_navigation unless a narrower source-backed node scope is introduced.
would_reject_if: A source shows charterparty_shipping_contracts is intentionally scoped only to cases that require portolan_charts_maritime_navigation.
The graph splits charterparty_shipping_contracts and reserves this id for a narrower technology that directly depends on portolan_charts_maritime_navigation.
A stronger source demonstrates that portolan_charts_maritime_navigation is chronologically and causally necessary for charterparty_shipping_contracts.
short_reason: A charterparty is a shipping contract and does not require portolan charts as a direct legal prerequisite.
